description_long: This is a single-story suburban house with a prominent gable roof in dark gray, featuring a combination of brick and light-colored siding on its exterior walls. The front facade includes a protruding bay window section and a double garage with a white door, while the main entrance is set back and partially obscured by the bay. Multiple rectangular windows are symmetrically arranged on the front and sides, providing ample natural light. The house is surrounded by a modest yard with several mature trees of varying species, adding greenery and shade. The rear of the house is simpler, with fewer windows and no visible entryway, while the sides show a mix of brick and siding. The overall design is practical and typical of North American suburban neighborhoods, emphasizing comfort and accessibility.
description: A single-story suburban house with a dark gable roof, brick and siding exterior, bay window, and attached double garage, surrounded by trees.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, bay window, double garage, and several trees in the yard.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the garage and house, with fewer windows and a simpler facade.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the brick facade, symmetrical windows, and a clear view of the yard's trees.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a single window on the rear wall and the arrangement of trees around the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main door with a small overhang, adjacent to a bay window and double garage, no porch or columns visible
